article_meta.title: Jurisprudence People Are Taking the Wrong Lesson From This Weekend's Dual Massacres By Ido Kirson and Austin Sarat Dec 1

reasoning: No attribution violations and several genuine author-prose bias instances were flagged, but the model used non-standard/incorrect bias type labels and missed multiple prominent bias elements in the author's connecting prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text comes from the authors’ own prose; no quoted or clearly attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged items reflect real advocacy, emotional appeals, and slanted framing in the authors’ voice; largely defensible as bias. One item leans on omission reasoning but remains plausible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several strong instances: subjective/emotional framing early (“horrific,” “rocked”), strawman/anticipation of opponents (“Surely, we will hear... That’s the wrong question”), categorical causal assertions (“Australia’s NFA works because...”), prescriptive advocacy (“It is clear... they need to use them. We need our own version...”), and predictive certainty (“it will only get worse”).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All labels used (Advocacy Bias, Selective Omission, Loaded Language, Partisan Framing) are outside the specified taxonomy; appropriate labels would include Opinion as Fact, Bias by Omission/Slant, Sensationalism/Emotionalism, Word Choice.
